text:
Staff on a Channel 9 broadcast team claimed they were assaulted when a group of people attempted to rob them in broad daylight, days after an Australian woman alleged she was "gang raped".

Australian tourists, Olympic athletes and television crews working in Paris for the Olympics have been told to be extra cautious following another alleged incident.

Two staff from the Channel 9 broadcast team were walking to their accommodation in the Le Bourget municipality on Monday afternoon when they were allegedly attacked.

They claimed a group of thieves attempted to steal one of their backpacks before the pair were then allegedly assaulted. The crew managed to get away safely.

The incident was reported to police, The Sydney Morning Herald reported.

It is the second incident involving Australians in less than two days, after a female tourist claimed she was "gang raped" by a group of men on Sunday (local time).

Vision emerged of the 25-year-old woman seeking help from bystanders at a kebab shop Boulevard de Clichy near the Moulin Rouge in Paris' 18th arrondissement.

Other customers tried to comfort the woman who was in tears with the dress she was wearing "backwards" and partially torn off, witnesses claimed.

The clip, obtained by 7News, also showed the Australian tourist signalling to a kebab shop owner that a man who walked in to the shop was one of her alleged attackers.

The staff member and the man got into a tussle before the latter fled.

Police later arrived at the scene and took statements, with the Australian woman telling officers she was raped by five men of African appearance.

Paris prosecutor's office said it is investigating the charge of gang rape "likely to have been committed on the night of July 19 to 20, 2024".

The attacks have prompted urgent warnings to all Australians in the City of Love, including athletes competing in the Olympic Games starting this week.

Australia’s chef de mission Anna Meares has been notified of the incident, but relayed that athletes had not flagged they felt unsafe in the city.

"Information we're passing onto our athletes is that security presence is really, really high and that's in order for them to act as quickly as possible to keep them safe," she said.

Competitors have been told by their security to wear plain clothes when they are outside the athletes village and sightseeing.

The Australian Olympic Committee have also been briefed.

The Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade said it is "making urgent enquiries" with French authorities following the reports of the alleged assault.

title: Two Channel 9 Olympic Games staff allegedly involved in attempted robbery in Paris days after Australian woman 'gang raped' | Sky News Australia
description: Australian tourists, Olympic athletes and television crews working in Paris for the Olympics have been told to be extra cautious following another alleged incident.
